chapter_title.en: Chapter 19 – The one upon whom the Answering is delayed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/83)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/83)
text: إِنَّ الْعَبْدَ لَيَدْعُو فَيَقُولُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لِلْمَلَكَيْنِ قَدِ اسْتَجَبْتُ لَهُ وَ لَكِنِ احْبِسُوهُ بِحَاجَتِهِ فَإِنِّي أُحِبُّ أَنْ أَسْمَعَ صَوْتَهُ وَ إِنَّ الْعَبْدَ لَيَدْعُو فَيَقُولُ اللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى عَجِّلُوا لَهُ حَاجَتَهُ فَإِنِّي أُبْغِضُ صَوْتَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Is’haq Bin Abu Hilal Al Madainy, from Hadeed,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The servant is supplicating, so Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic is Saying to the two Angels: “I<sup>azwj</sup> have Answered for him, but withhold his need, for I<sup>azwj</sup> Love to Hear his voice”; and the servant is supplicating, so Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Blessed and High is Saying: “Hasten his need for him, for I<sup>azwj</sup> Hate Hearing his voice”’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Ishaq ibn abu Hilal al-Mada’Ini from Hadid from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “When a servant prays, Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, says to the two angels, ‘I have accepted his prayer, but keep it on hold; I love to hear his voice.’ It may also happen that a servant prays, but Allah, the Most Blessed, the Most High, says, ‘Hurry up and help him quickly; I hate his voice.’”
